grounding_span: The initial proposal is that data readiness should be split into three bands. Each band being represented by a letter, A, B and C.
The initial proposal is that data readiness should be split into three bands. Each band being represented by a letter, A, B and C.
The initial proposal is that data readiness should be split into three bands. Each band being represented by a letter, A, B and C.
These bands reflect stages of data readiness which would each likely have some sub-levels, so the best data would be A1 and the worst data might be C4.
Thirdly, awareness of the transition from Band B to Band A (data moving to its context) would have made us realize that the question may well evolve and be more responsive to that outcome.
The lowest sub-level of Band C (let’s label it as C4) would represent a belief that the data may exist, but its existence isn’t even verified.
For data to arrive at C1, then it would have all these considerations dealt with.
By the end of Band B, when data is B1, a broad idea of limitations in the data should be present in the expert’s mind.
Once data has been considered alongside a task and any remedial steps have been taken, then the data is in A1 condition.
Tukey’s approach of “Exploratory Data Analysis” also fits within Band B.
Some parts of Band C are sometimes referred to as “data munging” or “data wrangling”, but those aren’t the only components of this band

AIDRIN utilizes the Pearson correlation coefficient to gauge the strength and direction of numerical feature correlations.

The representation rate assesses the distributions of different sensitive attributes in the dataset.

The statistical rate, on the other hand, evaluates fairness through conditional probabilities, ensuring that the target representations do not discriminate certain groups based on sensitive attributes.
This metric goes beyond binary groups by considering the average differences across all possible subgroups for a given target.
The overall FAIR compliance score is presented as a percentage, reflecting the proportion of fulfilled checks out of the total possible checks.
AIDRIN uses the ID as the chosen metric to measure class imbalance.
This metric provides a numerical representation of the disparity between the majority class instances and minority class instances in a dataset.
It uses the likelihood ratio (LR) test, offering a fine measurement of imbalance by comparing the existing class distribution to a balanced distribution.
Shapley values provide a means to quantify the impact of each feature on a model’s predictions.

The Cisco AI Readiness Index assesses organizations against six pillars of readiness—Strategy, Infrastructure, Data, Governance, Talent, and Culture—measured across 49 indicators.
Pillar scores were then combined to calculate an overall AI readiness score for each organization.
The pillar weightings are as follows: Strategy (15%); Infrastructure (25%); Data (20%); Governance (15%); Talent (15%); and Culture (10%).
